title: Gold Steadies as Traders Weigh Diplomatic Push to End Iran War
summary: Gold was little changed as traders weighed the latest efforts by the US and Iran to find a negotiated solution to the two-month war that’s choked energy supplies and heightened inflation risks.

title: Goldman Hikes Oil Forecasts Again as Hormuz Shock Builds
summary: Daan Struyven, Goldman Sachs Co-Head of Global Commodities Research, joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss his team's latest oil outlook. Struyven and his team upgraded Brent to $90/barrel by the fourth quarter, citing lower Persian Gulf production, and ongoing Strait of Hormuz disruption. They now assume a normalization in Gulf exports by the end of June (vs. a prior forecast of mid-May) and see a slower recovery for Gulf production. However, a global recession is not in the team's base case unless the strait remains mostly closed in a "severely adverse" scenario.  Struyven speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rising Yield Outlook Makes Japan’s Life Insurers Go Slow on JGBs
summary: The likelihood of further interest rate hikes in Japan is keeping many of its life insurers from actively purchasing the nation’s government bonds this year.

title: Pre-War Playbook Returns as Stock Traders Favor Asia Over US
summary: Investors are reverting to a pre-war playbook of betting Asian stocks will outpace US peers, as confidence builds in the region’s central role in the artificial-intelligence boom.

title: Oil Steadies as US Weighs Iran Proposal With Hormuz Still Shut
summary: Oil held a gain as traders weighed the next steps toward peace talks over the Iran war, with the US discussing a proposal from Tehran while the crucial Strait of Hormuz remained almost impassable.

title: Asian Stocks Look Subdued Ahead of Tech Earnings: Markets Wrap
summary: Asian stocks were poised for a soft open Tuesday as investors braced for a wave of earnings from tech giants while keeping a close eye on developments in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Vietnam Gas Major Looks to US as Iran War Reorders LPG Flows
summary: PetroVietnam Gas JSC plans to import more liquefied petroleum gas from the US than its traditional Middle East suppliers next month, in the latest sign that the Iran war is reordering global energy flows.

title: Spelman's Roz Brewer: Need to Run Colleges Like a Business
summary: Former Cam's Club CEO and Spelman College Interim President Roz Brewer discusses launching a $500 million 'Spelman Forward' campaign aimed at helping students prepare for their future after graduation, and her focus on creating the best eduction for black women in the country. She talks with Romaine Bostick for "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Mexico Arrests Cartel Boss With $5 Million US Bounty on His Head
summary: Mexico arrested a senior underworld figure with a $5 million bounty on his head who was sought for crimes including drug trafficking and defrauding US pensioners.

title: American Airlines Sells $1.14 Billion in Bonds for 32 Planes
summary: American Airlines Group Inc. on Monday sold $1.14 billion of bonds financing a group of planes.

title: Intel $6.5 Billion Bond Sale Signals Investor Hope in Turnaround
summary: Intel Corp. has been suffering from credit rating downgrades in recent years as its profits deteriorated, but on Monday bond investors signaled they are buying into the company’s turnaround story.

title: LifeMD Bone Longevity Lead on Osteoporosis Treatment, Prevention
summary: Dr. Doug Lucas, LifeMD Orthopedic Surgeon and Bone Health & Longevity Clinical Lead, joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss bone health for men and women, diagnosing and treating osteoporosis, how to prevent bone health issues from beginning and compounding, and more. He speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The Stock Market’s 'Curious Exuberance' Despite the Iran War
summary: On today’s Big Take podcast, Asia equities reporter Winnie Hsu and markets reporter Ruth Carson tell host Sarah Holder why global markets are looking past the Iran war — and why that could change. Listen wherever you get your podcasts. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Falls as Traders Weigh Restart of US-Iran Peace Talks
summary: Gold fell as efforts to resume peace talks between the US and Iran remained at an impasse, two months into a war that’s upended global markets and raised inflation risks.

title: Emerging Stocks Reclaim Pre-War Peak on AI, Hormuz Deal Hopes
summary: Emerging-market stocks climbed Monday amid a renewed focus on artificial intelligence companies, even as the stalemate between the US and Iran on resuming peace negotiations left the Strait of Hormuz almost impassable and pushed oil higher.

title: Hut 8 Raises $3.25 Billion to Help Fund Google-Tied Data Center
summary: A Hut 8 Corp. project sold $3.25 billion of investment-grade bonds Monday to fund the construction of a data center tied to Alphabet Inc.’s Google, the latest in a string of borrowing by firms spearheading the artificial intelligence boom.

title: Private Credit Boom Pushes Fund Finance Market Past $1 Trillion
summary: The fund finance market has surged past $1 trillion, according to a pair of new reports, as money managers borrow more to manage liquidity and bridge delayed exits.

title: Sotheby's US Private Sales Head on $130M Mnuchin Auction
summary: Sotheby's Head of Private Sales Courtney Kremers discusses the sale of 'unique' art world figure Robert Mnuchin's $130 million collection, and the shift towards younger buyers in the marketplace. She talks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifled on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Starboard Builds Stake in Flowserve, Push for Changes
summary: Activist investor Starboard Value has built a significant stake in Flowserve Corp. and is speaking to the industrial company about potential changes, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Stocks Hold Record in Countdown to Tech Earnings: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks held at record highs before a raft of megacap earnings that will test whether April’s rally is sustainable, with traders also awaiting updates from major central banks on the economic fallout of the Iran war.

title: S&P, Nasdaq Continue Record Run | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Grupo Mexico to Merge Power Unit With BlackRock-Backed Saavi
summary: Mexican mining and railroad conglomerate Grupo Mexico has struck a deal to merge its electricity generation operations with a BlackRock Inc. unit, creating the country’s largest private power operator.

title: High Bar for Tech Earnings This Week: Covley
summary: Manning & Napier Senior Investment Analyst Kelly Covley says there will be a high bar this week for tech companies to justify their level of AI capex spending. She speaks to Bloomberg's Matt Miller on 'Open Interest.' (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rogers Makes 10,000 Employees Eligible for Buyouts to Cut Costs
summary: Rogers Communications Inc., Canada’s largest wireless company, is making about 10,000 employees eligible for voluntary buyouts as the telecommunications industry deals with major growth and debt challenges.

title: A Warsh-Led Fed Stands to Reignite the Treasuries Steepener Bet
summary: Wall Street strategists say that a Kevin Warsh-led Federal Reserve may jolt the $31 trillion Treasuries market out of its narrow trading ranges, urging investors to position for shorter-dated yields to eventually move lower.

title: Need For 'Clean Beta' with ETFs: F/M Investments CEO
summary: Alex Morris, CEO & CIO of F/M Investments, joins Scarlet Fu and Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Banks to Join BMO-Led $1.8 Billion Debt Deal for Honeywell Spinoff
summary: Bank of Montreal’s investment banking unit is leading a $1.8 billion debt deal supporting the sale of Honeywell International Inc.’s productivity solutions and services business to industrial manufacturer Brady Corp.

title: Shell’s ARC Deal Seen as Win for Mark Carney’s Pro-Oil Pivot
summary: Shell Plc.’s acquisition of Canada’s ARC Resources Ltd. marks a vote of confidence in Prime Minister Mark Carney’s push to expand the nation’s hydrocarbon exports beyond the traditional US market, analysts say.

title: Walmart Plans to Sell Investment-Grade Bonds on Monday
summary: Walmart Inc. raised $4.25 billion from a high-grade bond offering, boosting the size of the deal amid a flood of issuance that was mostly well received.

title: Potential 'GRFT' ETF Outperforms S&P 500
summary: Bloomberg Intelligence's Athanasios Psarofagis joins Scarlet Fu and Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg ETF IQ.". An ETF designed to track White House Policy has been in limbo for more than a year, as exchanges steer clear of what looks like a high-volatility, politically sensitive trade. The potential GRFT ETF is easily outperforming the S&P 500 this year. (Source: Bloomberg)
